vpc_findings.description: VPC vpc-03aa5903ef730af4e has no VPC Flow Logs enabled — network traffic is unaudited
Default VPC detected — resources should use dedicated VPCs with custom network design

network_acl_findings.description: Rule #100 allows ALL outbound traffic from 0.0.0.0/0 — no network-layer restriction in place. This is AWS's unmodified default rule — normal in most environments that rely on Security Groups as the primary access control.

Rule #100 allows ALL inbound traffic from 0.0.0.0/0 — no network-layer restriction in place. This is AWS's unmodified default rule — normal in most environments that rely on Security Groups as the primary access control.

iam_findings.description: Policy 'AWSElasticBeanstalkManagedUpdatesCustomerRolePolicy' grants iam:passrole — known privilege-escalation primitive(s). A principal with this permission can potentially grant itself broader access later.
Role 'aisec-readonly-role' allows cross-account assumption from arn:aws:iam::315527911599:root without an sts:ExternalId condition — vulnerable to confused-deputy risk if that account ID leaks.
